content: Hi, whats the correct way to transfer a &str to char* (null-terminated c string)??
For char * that's null-terminated you generally want to use the CStr type, which has this as an invariant.

Alex Gaynor said:

For char * that's null-terminated you generally want to use the CStr type, which has this as an invariant.

what If we want to convert &str to CStr? I don't see the interface.
Does it mean we should always use CStr instead of &str or String??

If you're trying to pass teh value as nul terminated to C, using CStr is the ideal solution, yes.

Otherwise you need to construct a string with a NUL terminator, since Rust &str doesn't have them

Got it, I will go with CStr.

Thank you :)
